reviews.review_msg: Professor Manson was a very good instructor IMO. She encouraged questions and interaction with students during her lectures which made them engaging. She referred to slides, not read verbatim, but used as prompts. All testing in-class, so felt fair. Heavy reading load and not a "read and regurgitate" course, but mind expanding if engage seriously.

Dr. Manson only reads off her slides and doesn't provide clear instructions of what her exams will be. There is not assignments outside of class as all her exams are in-person either in class or in tutorials. She often asks her students to answer questions but if you answer wrong she answers in a passive aggressive manner. Wouldn't take again.
Absolutely the worst class I have ever taken in my life. This is class is bane of my existence. I dont ever wanna have a single philosophical thought because of this course.
This class is cooked do not take it

Her lectures are very engaging, and she usually asks questions to the class to keep people engaged. She can seem a bit intimidating. I only dropped this course because the TA I had was a really tough grader, especially with wording. All of the assignments are done in class.
Cool introduction to philosophy, covers a lot of ground. If you go to lecture and do the readings/take notes you should do well.

Review for PHIL 322 - Michaela has a tendency to be abrasive. Quizzes/Tests about specific points in readings were not a constructive way to learn Philosophy. Deconstructed paper was not super helpful, and feedback was not delivered for the last couple submissions. Hard to correct details from the last submission without feedback.

Kept us late twice. The 'deconstructed paper' won't teach you anything if you've ever written a paper, and will teach you 1/2 of what you need if you haven't. Spoke disrespectfully to a guy who hadn't done an assignment on time and was asking about her late policy Told us she hadn't bothered to think of one, and never did. Not a bad lecturer.

You can tell she really cares about her students and about philosophy. This was a lecture style course, so lectures are expected, but she does answer student questions. If you do the readings and pay attention the evaluations are easy.

Michaela is a very competent lecturer who made intrinsically dry and difficult content thought-provoking and inspiring. The classes were run very well, and she struck a good balance between lecturing and facilitating group discussion. She is a tough marker, but provides very valuable feedback. I would highly recommend her class.
